description: In this appeal, typically addressed to the community ('your excellencies, my masters, the illustrious lordly judges, and...the elders of Israel'), a widow and mother of four, weighed down by debt, asks for 'something to conceal myself (astur bihi nafs) and the fo[ur] who are with me. She thus voices the common plaint of the master, the 'concealed,' who strives to maintain him/herself economically without having to 'uncover his/her face, especially by resorting to the public dole. (Information from Goitein's index cards and from Cohen)
tags: Appeal, debt, charity, Women's letters, communal
description_he: עצומה לקהילה מאלמנה ואם לארבעה, טבועה בחובות, המבקשת 'משהו שאכסה בו את עצמי ואת ה[ארבעה] שאתי'. מבטאת את הקושי של מי שמנסה לעמוד על עצמו כלכלית ללא נדבה.
